2605.25891.title: Causal Tongue-Tie: LLMs Can Encode Causal Direction, But Their Yes/No Outputs Fail to Express
2605.25891.paper_summary: We find a mismatch between what large language models encode about a causal question and what they answer. On anti-commonsense CLadder items, a fixed linear probe recovers the evidence-supported answer from the model's hidden state (accuracy approximately 0.97), while the spoken Yes/No reverts to the commonsense one (accuracy approximately 0.5). We call this approximately +0.5 gap Causal Tongue-Tie: a wrong Yes/No decomposes into two separable failure modes: no internal signal versus a signal the verbal interface cannot say. The implication cuts both ways for output-only causal benchmarks: a benchmark "correct" need not mean the model has understood, and a benchmark "wrong" need not mean it cannot. Sweeping claims about whether LLMs can do causal reasoning, drawn from a single accuracy number, deserve a second look.
2605.25891.paper_summary_zh: 我們發現大型語言模型對於因果問題的編碼與其回答之間存在不匹配。在反常識的 CLadder 項目中，固定的線性探針從模型的隱藏狀態中恢復出證據支持的答案（準確率約為 0.97），而口頭的 Yes/No 則回歸到常識答案（準確率約為 0.5）。我們將這個約 +0.5 的差距稱為因果語言障礙：錯誤的 Yes/No 可以分解為兩種可分離的失敗模式：沒有內部信號與口頭介面無法表達的信號。這一含義對於僅輸出的因果基準來說是雙向的：一個基準的「正確」不一定意味著模型已經理解，而一個基準的「錯誤」不一定意味著它無法理解。關於 LLM 是否能進行因果推理的廣泛聲明，若僅根據單一的準確率數字，值得重新審視。
2605.25891.author: Ziyi Ding et.al.
